title: agrostis canina var. coarctata
intro: Agrostis canina var. coarctata, also known as a variety of red bentgrass, is a type of grass that belongs to the Poaceae family and is commonly found in temperate regions. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it a popular choice for treating wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of Agrostis canina var. coarctata include its ability to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing, as well as its potential to soothe digestive issues and alleviate respiratory problems. In herbal preparations, the plant is often harvested, dried, and used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are then consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
